name: High blood pressure
precautions: Check blood pressure regularly.
Bring readings to a clinician.
Seek urgent care for readings around or above 180/120 or severe symptoms.
diet: DASH-style low-sodium meals.
Vegetables, fruits, whole grains, beans, nuts, low-fat dairy if tolerated.
Limit salty packaged foods, processed meat, fast food, and sugary drinks.

name: Diabetes
precautions: Track glucose, meals, symptoms, and medications.
Do not change insulin or diabetes medicine without clinician guidance.
Urgent care for confusion, vomiting, severe weakness, or very high/low glucose.
diet: Consistent meal timing.
Non-starchy vegetables, beans, lentils, controlled portions of whole grains, lean protein.
Limit sugary drinks, large refined-carb portions, and sweets.

name: High cholesterol
precautions: Review lipid labs with a clinician.
Ask about family history and heart risk.
Do not stop cholesterol medication without clinician guidance.
diet: Mediterranean or DASH-style pattern.
Oats, beans, vegetables, fruits, nuts, fish if eaten.
Limit saturated fat, trans fat, fried foods, fatty red meat, and processed meat.

name: Heart disease
precautions: Emergency care for chest pain, shortness of breath, fainting, or blue lips.
Track symptoms, weight changes if heart failure, and medications.
Follow clinician activity limits.
diet: Mediterranean or DASH-style pattern.
Low sodium if advised.
Limit fried foods, processed meat, excess saturated fat, and high-salt foods.

name: Asthma
precautions: Know triggers and keep an asthma action plan.
Use prescribed controller/rescue medicines as directed.
Emergency care if breathing is severe or rescue medicine is not helping.
diet: No single asthma diet replaces asthma treatment.
Balanced meals, hydration, fruits and vegetables.
Avoid known food allergies and reflux-triggering foods if they worsen symptoms.

name: COPD
precautions: Avoid smoking and air pollution.
Use inhalers/oxygen only as prescribed.
Emergency care for blue lips, confusion, or severe breathing trouble.
diet: Small balanced meals if breathing is harder after large meals.
Adequate protein and hydration unless restricted.
Limit excess salt if swelling or heart disease is present.

name: Chronic kidney disease
precautions: Nutrition depends on kidney stage and labs.
Avoid NSAIDs or supplements unless clinician-approved.
Track blood pressure, diabetes control, labs, and medications.
diet: Kidney-modified diet with clinician/dietitian guidance.
Often sodium control; potassium, phosphorus, protein, and fluid may need individual limits.
Do not use generic high-protein, high-potassium, or DASH plans without kidney review.

name: Obesity or weight management
precautions: Screen for eating-disorder risk.
Choose sustainable changes over extreme dieting.
Review medications and medical causes with a clinician when appropriate.
diet: Balanced plate with vegetables, protein, high-fiber carbs, and water.
Limit sugary drinks, ultra-processed foods, and frequent fried foods.
Use culturally familiar, affordable meals.
doctor: Primary care, registered dietitian, obesity medicine if needed

name: Acid reflux / GERD
precautions: Emergency care for chest pain with heart attack symptoms.
Clinician review for trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, or weight loss.
Avoid lying down soon after meals.
diet: Smaller meals.
Limit personal triggers such as spicy foods, fatty meals, chocolate, peppermint, caffeine, or alcohol.
Avoid late heavy meals.

name: Fatty liver disease
precautions: Review liver tests and alcohol use with a clinician.
Manage diabetes, cholesterol, and weight if present.
Avoid unnecessary supplements that may harm the liver.
diet: Mediterranean-style eating.
Weight-management pattern if appropriate.
Limit sugary drinks, excess alcohol, refined carbs, and ultra-processed foods.

name: Anemia
precautions: Find the cause before taking high-dose supplements.
Urgent care for chest pain, fainting, severe shortness of breath, or black stool.
Ask about heavy bleeding or GI bleeding.
diet: Iron-rich foods such as beans, lentils, leafy greens, fortified grains, eggs, fish, poultry, or lean meats depending preference.
Pair plant iron with vitamin C foods.
Discuss B12/folate needs for vegan/vegetarian users.
doctor: Primary care, hematology if severe or unclear

name: Depression
precautions: Emergency/crisis support for suicidal thoughts or self-harm risk.
Encourage therapy, primary care, or mental-health care.
Do not stop psychiatric medication without clinician guidance.
diet: Regular meals and hydration.
Mediterranean-style pattern may support overall health.
Limit alcohol and avoid using substances to cope.

name: Anxiety
precautions: Emergency care for chest pain, fainting, or trouble breathing that could be medical.
Consider therapy or primary care if persistent.
Avoid excessive caffeine if it worsens symptoms.
diet: Regular meal timing.
Hydration.
Limit caffeine, alcohol, and heavy late meals if they worsen symptoms.

name: Insomnia
precautions: Seek care if persistent, severe, or linked to depression/anxiety.
Avoid mixing sleep medicines with alcohol.
Do not start sedatives without clinician guidance.
diet: Limit late caffeine and heavy late meals.
Keep regular meal and sleep timing.
Avoid alcohol as a sleep aid.
doctor: Primary care, behavioral health, sleep medicine

name: Arthritis / joint pain
precautions: Clinician review for swollen hot joint, fever, injury, or sudden severe pain.
Use safe movement and joint protection.
Ask before frequent NSAID use, especially with kidney/stomach/heart issues.
diet: Mediterranean-style anti-inflammatory pattern.
Weight-management support if joint load is a concern.
Limit ultra-processed foods and excess sugary drinks.

name: Gout
precautions: Clinician review for sudden severe joint swelling, fever, or first attack.
Do not change gout medication without clinician guidance.
Hydrate unless fluid-restricted.
diet: Limit alcohol, sugary drinks, organ meats, and large portions of red meat/seafood.
Emphasize water, vegetables, whole grains, and low-fat dairy if tolerated.
Use caution with fish-heavy diets.

name: Migraine / recurrent headache
precautions: Emergency care for sudden worst headache, confusion, weakness, stiff neck, fever, or head injury.
Track triggers and medication use.
Avoid overusing pain medicines.
diet: Regular meals and hydration.
Limit individual triggers such as alcohol or skipped meals.
Do not use extreme elimination diets without guidance.

name: Pregnancy care
precautions: Emergency care for heavy bleeding, fainting, severe abdominal pain, severe headache, vision changes, or trouble breathing.
Use prenatal care and medication review.
Avoid alcohol and unsafe foods.
diet: Balanced prenatal eating with folic acid/iron guidance from clinician.
Food safety: avoid high-mercury fish and risky unpasteurized foods.
Manage nausea/hydration with clinician support if severe.

name: PCOS
precautions: Review irregular periods, fertility goals, diabetes risk, and symptoms with clinician.
Do not self-prescribe hormones.
Screen for mood and metabolic issues.
diet: Balanced high-fiber meals.
Protein with meals, controlled refined carbs, vegetables, whole grains.
Weight-neutral or weight-management plan depending patient goals and clinician guidance.

name: Hypothyroidism
precautions: Use thyroid medication exactly as prescribed.
Do not change dose without lab review.
Discuss supplements because iron/calcium can affect absorption timing.
diet: Balanced diet with adequate iodine from normal food sources unless restricted.
Avoid extreme thyroid diets.
Take medication timing instructions seriously.

name: Celiac disease
precautions: Confirm diagnosis before starting lifelong restriction when possible.
Avoid gluten if diagnosed.
Review nutrient deficiencies and cross-contamination.
diet: Strict gluten-free diet for diagnosed celiac disease.
Naturally gluten-free foods: rice, potatoes, corn, beans, fruits, vegetables, plain meats/fish/eggs if eaten.
Check labels and avoid wheat, barley, rye.

name: Irritable bowel syndrome
precautions: Clinician review for blood in stool, weight loss, fever, anemia, or nighttime symptoms.
Track triggers.
Do not over-restrict food without dietitian help.
diet: Regular meals and hydration.
Fiber adjustment depending constipation/diarrhea.
Low-FODMAP trial only with guidance if needed.

name: Osteoporosis
precautions: Fall prevention.
Review fracture risk and medications.
Ask clinician before calcium/vitamin D supplements if kidney stones or CKD.
diet: Adequate calcium and vitamin D from food/supplements if advised.
Protein and strength-supporting nutrition.
Limit excess alcohol and avoid smoking.

name: Food allergy
precautions: Emergency care for breathing trouble, swelling, dizziness, or widespread hives.
Carry prescribed epinephrine if advised.
Read labels and avoid known allergens.
diet: Avoid confirmed allergens.
Use safe substitutes to keep nutrition complete.
Dietitian/allergist support for multiple allergies.

name: Flu/COVID/respiratory infection
precautions: Emergency care for trouble breathing, chest pain, confusion, blue lips, or inability to stay awake.
Rest, hydration, isolation precautions when contagious.
Ask clinician about antivirals if high-risk.
diet: Hydration.
Small nourishing meals if appetite is low.
Avoid alcohol and dehydration.
doctor: Primary care, urgent care if worsening

name: Pneumonia
precautions: Emergency care for breathing trouble, blue lips, chest pain, confusion, or low oxygen.
Do not use leftover antibiotics.
Follow clinician treatment and vaccination guidance.
diet: Hydration unless fluid-restricted.
Balanced meals with protein as tolerated.
Small meals if breathing is difficult.
doctor: Primary care, urgent care, pulmonology if severe

name: Stroke recovery
precautions: New stroke symptoms are always emergency symptoms.
Follow rehab and medication plan.
Address swallowing safety if affected.
diet: DASH or Mediterranean-style eating.
Texture-modified diet if swallowing problems, guided by speech/swallow team.
Limit sodium and saturated fat.

name: Cancer support
precautions: Fever during chemotherapy can be urgent.
Do not take supplements without oncology approval.
Report severe vomiting, dehydration, bleeding, or infection signs.
diet: High-protein, calorie-adequate meals if losing weight.
Food safety during immune suppression.
Manage nausea with small meals and oncology dietitian guidance.

name: Lactose intolerance
precautions: Clinician review for weight loss, blood in stool, anemia, or severe symptoms.
Avoid unnecessary restriction without diagnosis.
Ensure calcium/vitamin D intake.
diet: Lactose-free dairy, fortified plant milks, yogurt if tolerated, calcium-rich foods.
Limit lactose-containing foods based on tolerance.
Read labels.

name: Anaphylaxis / severe allergic reaction
precautions: Call emergency services for breathing trouble, swelling, dizziness, or widespread hives.
Use prescribed epinephrine if available.
Avoid known triggers and get allergy follow-up.
diet: Strictly avoid known food allergens.
Use safe alternatives.
Dietitian support if multiple foods are removed.
